dailyJudgementSnippet: Anthropic won because Claude and Claude Code kept resurfacing in coding-agent threads as the tool people actually reach for, with commenters calling it the best current option and useful enough to rebuild similar projects around, even though one JJ-related thread complained about worktree confusion and fallbacks to git [E1][E2][E3].
winnerExplanation: Anthropic had the strongest day on balance: it showed up in multiple separate front-page discussions with concrete product-level commentary, and the positive signals were practical rather than abstract. HN commenters described Claude Code as the current best coding assistant despite bugs, referenced active use around agent tooling, and treated it as capable enough to reproduce apps quickly [E1][E2]. The main drag was one thread saying Claude Desktop and related agents stumble on JJ/worktree workflows [E3], but that did not outweigh the broader sense that Anthropic's coding product is the one users are defaulting to today.

openai.judgementSnippet: Codex got a specific favorable nod for running commands inside an OS-provided sandbox, but that was the day's only meaningful OpenAI-specific signal, so the uplift stayed modest [E4].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Claude Code was repeatedly cited as the practical coding tool people reach for, including comments that it is still the best option today and useful enough to clone similar apps, though one JJ thread said Claude Desktop/worktree behavior breaks that workflow and falls back to git too often [E1][E2][E3].

google_gemini.judgementSnippet: Google AI drew pointed complaints about missing tool-call streaming, lacking a local tokenizer, and AI Studio making extra count_tokens calls, and a separate Google agent-systems post was met with skepticism about both the paper and the broader product experience beyond direct Gemini API use [E5][E6].

evidence.summary: In the coding-agent thread, commenters said 'Claude Code seems best despite its bugs' and praised its generous subscription and real-world usefulness for agent workflows.
On NanoClaw, commenters said they often rebuild similar apps with Claude Code and mentioned tooling that can hook directly into Claude Code, signaling strong practical adoption.
A VisualJJ commenter said Claude Desktop's worktree behavior breaks with JJ and that Claude and other agents get confused and fall back to git too often.
A commenter singled out Codex for using OS-provided sandboxing and argued that its safety measures do not make the agent 'mostly useless,' giving OpenAI one concrete positive mention.

In the coding-agent discussion, a commenter criticized Google AI tooling for not supporting tool-call streaming and not even offering a local tokenizer, saying AI Studio burns API calls on count_tokens.
The Google agent-systems research post drew skeptical comments calling the paper shallow, and one commenter said they were unimpressed with Google's AI products apart from direct Gemini API calls.
